Embark on a fully guided adventure that showcases the awe-inspiring beauty and deep cultural heritage of Mt. Tarawera. This unforgettable walking journey not only reveals dramatic volcanic landscapes but also connects you with the rich Māori history of the region. Learn the stories of the mountain's spiritual significance to local iwi (tribes) while taking in breathtaking 360° panoramic views. Perfect for all ages, this unique experience offers an optional run into the heart of the volcanic crater, providing an exhilarating touch to your cultural immersion. Whether you're a nature lover, history enthusiast, or adrenaline seeker, Mt. Tarawera is sure to leave you inspired and amazed!

Activity details

🌊✨ Experience the Spirit of the Whanganui Awa (River) on a 3-Day Guided River Journey with Owhango Adventures! ✨🌊

Imagine gliding down the majestic Whanganui River, surrounded by breathtaking landscapes and the rich tapestry of Māori culture. Join us for an unforgettable 3-Day, 2-Night Guided and Catered River Journey, where you will be led by local Kaitiaki (guides), the guardians of the land and river, who are tangata whenua (people of the land).

In a traditional 6-person waka (canoe) or traditional 2-person Canadian style canoe, you will not only experience the serenity of the river but also connect deeply with the stories, traditions, and wisdom of the Māori people. Each paddle stroke will take you through lush native bush, past stunning cliffs, and into the heart of a culture that has thrived for centuries.

🍃 What to Expect:

Culturally Enriching Experiences: Engage with local customs and stories that bring the landscape to life.
Stunning Scenery: Discover the natural beauty of Aotearoa, from tranquil waters to lush riverbanks.
Community Connection: Bu
highlights: Free night accommodation at our Base in Owhango the night prior to your journey departure.
Rapid Water Flow: The Whanganui River features various sections with different water flow speeds. Sudden changes in flow can present challenges, especially for novice paddlers.
Obstacles: The river is home to natural obstacles such as fallen trees, rocks, and shallow areas. Navigating these hazards requires skill and awareness.
Weather Conditions: Weather can change rapidly in this region. Rain can lead to increased river flows and potential flooding. Always check the forecast before heading out.
Wildlife Encounters: While the river is home to beautiful wildlife, encounters with animals can pose risks. Be cautious and respectful of their habitats.
Hypothermia: Even in warmer months, the temperature of the water can be quite cold. Proper gear is essential to mitigate the risk of hypothermia.
Fatigue: Paddling can be physically demanding, especially on longer trips. It’s important to pace yourself and take regular breaks to avoid exhaustion.
Navigation Skills: Understanding how to read river maps and navigation skills are crucial. Being lost or disoriented can lead to dangerous situations.

Activity details

Our 1.5 hour Wildlife & Penguin tour offers the most exclusive opportunity to view a variety of wildlife within our private eco-reserve. The reserve is home to New Zealand fur seals, sea lions, hoiho (yellow-eyed penguins), kororā (little penguins), forest birds, seabirds, and much more while contributing to conservation.
Your journey begins in a theatre setting, where you’ll learn about our dedicated conservation work to protect the critically endangered hoiho (Yellow-eyed Penguin), alongside efforts to restore the native flora and fauna. From there, a short bus ride takes you into the heart of the reserve, where you’ll embark on a guided nature walk of approximately 2 kilometres (1.25 miles). Along the way, you never know what you might see—each day offers a unique experience.

Hoiho are the rarest penguin species in the world, with fewer than 500 individuals remaining
on mainland New Zealand—and their population is continuing to decline. While sightings vary throughout the year, your tour includes a visit to our rehabilitation centre (when penguins are in care),

Kia ora Te Araroa Trail Hikers!

After weeks of trekking Aotearoa’s wild heart, it’s time to rest those legs and experience one of New Zealand’s Great Walks — by canoe!
Swap your hiking boots for a paddle and drift down the legendary Whanganui River on an unforgettable 5-Day / 4-Night journey from Whakahoro to Whanganui.

🌊 Why Join This Adventure?

Rest & Recover: Let the current carry you while your legs enjoy a well-earned break.

Stunning Campsites: Sleep under star-filled skies beside the tranquil river at remote DOC and private sites.

Rich Culture & Nature: Paddle through lush bush, encounter native birds, and feel the living spirit of the Whanganui — an ancestral river steeped in Māori history.

🏕️ Journey Overview

Your scenic voyage begins at Whakahoro, winding through deep-green valleys and past iconic landmarks such as the Bridge to Nowhere — a powerful reminder of early settlers’ hopes and hardships.
Each paddle stroke takes you deeper into the serenity of Whanganui National Park, finishing in the riverside city of Whanganui.

Your cruise will begin at one of the most scenic points of the journey. You can take in the magnificent view of Mitre Peak (New Zealand's most photographed mountain) and the Sinbad Gully. This place is home to the Kakapo (world's heaviest flightless parrot), which was once thought to be extinct but was rediscovered in 1948.

From there, you will cruise past seal colonies and feel the spray of the waterfall as you travel directly under the Fairy Falls. Once the cruise reaches the Tasman Sea, you will get to see the hidden entrance to the Milford Sound (which Captain Cook missed twice on his explorations). Activity details

Arrive at Owhango Adventures Base where there is safe and secure parking for your vehicle while you cycle the famous 42 Traverse.

Intermediate: Grade 3

The 42 Traverse is a challenging track best suited to experienced, well-equipped people. It leads through fantastic native forest and the awesome Canyon valley. There are superb views back to the volcanoes of Tongariro National Park.

The route is an old logging road that gets its name from the original 'State Forest 42'. It is popular for a range of activities: mountain biking, quad and trail biking, four-wheel driving (summer only), horse riding, fishing, hunting and hiking.

The area is remote and there are often slips, fallen trees and other damage on the track. Sections of the track are very technical. Read the Know Before You Go section carefully for information on what to pack and expect.

The 42 Traverse route: from Kapoors Road to Ohinetonga Road
The track can be used in either direction. However, it is easier for bikers to go from Kapoors Road to Ohinetonga Road, as there is more downhill that way.

The track starts at the end of Kapoors Road, off SH47. The track leads north. There are many intersecting tracks and old logging roads. Look carefully at intersections and make sure you are staying on the right route. Carry a m

Sit back and enjoy the scenery as our sturdy boat explores the stunningly beautiful and rugged coastline near the entrance of the Otago Harbour. Our crew will give you a full commentary during your cruise about our local wildlife, history and geology.

The trip will travel past the beautiful Aramoana Beach and it’s tidal sandflats. We often spot sea lions here, sprawled out, basking in the sun. These impressive animals can weigh almost half a tonne. After nearly being wiped out by early settlers they have recently started returning to the sandy beaches around the southern coastline of New Zealand. The sealion population is now growing steadily.

Across the other side of the harbour channel you’ll see remnants of the areas military history. Several lookout posts and gun emplacements were built in to the rocky hill, connected by tunnels. They are now a popular spot for local fisherman.

You’ll see uninterrupted views of the world's only mainland colony of royal albatross. We want to get you c

Activity details

Take a wonderful journey back in time, on a guided tour of New Zealand's castle.

Built in 1871, it took two hundred workers roughly three years to build the exterior shell and a further twelve years for master European craftsmen to finish the interior. Over the years the castle and grounds fell in to ruin and disrepair. In 1967 the property was bought by the Barker family who have spent many years restoring it to its former grandeur. The rooms are once again filled with original artworks, clothing and furniture.

During the tour you’ll see the magnificent carved ceilings, antiques and painstakingly restored original features. Hear the scandalous and tragic history of the original owner, William Larnach who took his own life with a single shot to the head. Climb the spiral staircase to the rooftop tower for stunning 360° views of the Otago Peninsula. We highly recommend a short walk around the beautiful gardens, recognized to be of international significance.

Time is allowed to explore the grounds or take tea in the Ballroom Cafe. You’ll then meet your guide

Next, you'll join our Wildlife Cruise, with it's uninterrupted views of the world’s only mainland nesting place of royal albatross. Watch as these huge birds glide in from the ocean and soar high above their hillside nest sites.

Your skipper will guide the boat close to the base of dramatic volcanic cliffs, with a historic lighthouse perched high above. Beneath the cliffs you’ll see fur seals play on rocky outcrops and swim in tidal pools.

A short cruise out at sea and you’ll spot rarely seen ocean birds including other albatross species, cormorants and petrels. We sometimes spot sea lions, blue penguins, dolphins and other marine mammals.

Whether you’re a family, group of mates, school crew, stag or hens do, or work team – this is your own private surf session. You’ll learn together, laugh together, and ride waves together. Whether you're a first-timer or a water-lover looking for a new challenge, our 2-hour Stand Up Paddleboard (SUP) Lesson & Tour offers the perfect blend of learning, balance, and breathtaking scenery.

Set on the stunning Mangawhai Harbour, this experience is fully customised to your group, whether it’s a solo session, a family day out, or a team adventure.

With expert instruction and top-of-the-line gear, you’ll quickly gain the skills and confidence to glide across the water. From learning the basics to exploring quiet estuaries and local wildlife, you’ll enjoy an unforgettable session that’s fun, safe, and suited to all ages 7+.

The Kaituna is a white water enthusiast's dream come true. 14 awesome drops, including two waterfalls and some other great rapids in a stunning jungle setting

Join the Original Kaituna Rafting Company for an unbeatable grade 5 white-water experience on one of New Zealand's most exciting and beautiful rivers!

Experience the thrills and spills of the mighty Kaituna River as we take on the world-famous 7-metre Tutea Falls, the highest commercially rafted waterfall.

Together with your expert guide, your team will enjoy a 50-minute action-packed ride down 14 epic rapids in a tight and twisting warm water jungle canyon.

This is a white water enthusiasts dream and is a must-do for adrenaline junkies and 1st-time rafters alike!

No prior experience is necessary as you will be given full and comprehensive training.

All you need is a reasonable level of fitness, a sense of fun and adventure & your swimwear.

We also rent towels for $5

Best of Both Worlds!!

Start your adventure with an exhilarating hour of Grade 5 Rafting on the mighty Kaituna River, home to the world’s highest rafted waterfall and 14 thrilling rapids. Afterward, indulge in the rejuvenating mud baths before unwinding in the naturally heated geothermal waters of the spectacular Hell's Gate hot pools!

Kaituna Cascades is the Original Kaituna Rafting Company starting back in 91.

Hell’s Gate is home to New Zealand's only geothermal mud bath experience and has been a cherished site for healing and rejuvenation among the Māori for over 800 years.

Activity details

Explore the outer coast of Pōhatu Marine Reserve by kayak and marvel at its spectacular towering cliffs, rock stacks, reefs, and deep-sea caves.

Flea Bay Island and the impressive island arch make an added exciting attraction in settled sea conditions. Observe the diverse wildlife and marine species such as penguins, seals, various sea birds and possibly the world’s smallest dolphin!

Best of all, no kayaking experience necessary! Trips are only run in settled, safe conditions by experienced and knowledgeable guides. Your safety is our top priority.

What's included

Fully guided by qualified and experienced staff
All kayaking gear (life jackets, dry jackets, spray deck, dry bags for cameras)

Additional information
Cancellations
highlights: Wear something comfortable: leggings or board shorts - jeans are not suitable - or something you can roll up for when we get in and out of the kayak.

Wear layers that you can add/remove if it is too hot or cold.
You might also bring a spare change of clothes and a towel just in case you get wet.
A pair of shoes that you won’t mind getting wet while walking on rocks, on the beach and stepping in and out of the kayaks.
A bottle of water
Sun protection: hat, sunglasses, sunscreen
Option 1: No further tours. Full Refund.

About the Tour

Are you ready to embark on a journey that will test your limits and awaken your sense of adventure?  Our full day canyon tour offers an unforgettable experience for the bold and fearless who crave excitement and challenge.

Your day starts with a pick up from Queenstown or Wanaka, then a 1-2 hour vehicle transfer out to the canyon, a chance to rest and prepare for the incredible day ahead.  Expect to see sparkling lakes and picturesque mountains, all with a coffee stop along the way!

Discover the wildest corners of Dunedin on land and sea!

Bus out from the city along the beautiful Otago Peninsula. Hear stories of sea creatures and early settlers as you pass quaint little townships and rolling farmland. You'll be presented with great views of the harbour and rugged coastline.

At Wellers Rock Wharf, you’ll board the Wildlife Cruise and travel out into the ocean, enjoying the stunning scenery as we search for royal albatross, fur seals and other unique Southern Ocean creatures.

name: Ale Trail – Wharehuanui Adventure
description:
Ride Arrowtown to Queenstown at your own pace with brewery stops along the way 🍺

Why this ride

Self-guided · Brewery stops · Shuttle included · Ride your own pace

Overview
Duration
5.5 Hours
Meeting point
Marmolada Café – 43 Camp Street (corner of Camp & Shotover Street), Queenstown
Group size
20 Max
Activity details

Ride at your own pace from Arrowtown to Queenstown, stopping at hand-picked breweries along the way.

This is a self-guided experience designed for flexibility — no strict schedule, no rush. Ride as far as you like, stop when you want, and enjoy the best of Queenstown’s food, beer, and scenery.

How it works:

• Meet in central Queenstown (Marmolada Café, Camp Street)
• Shuttle to Arrowtown and quick bike briefing
• Ride the scenic Wharehuanui Trail at your own pace
• Stop at breweries and cafés along the way
• Finish where you like and return by shuttle (or ride back)

Stops along the way include:

• Arrowtown – optional brunch stop to start your day
• Gantley’s Tavern
• Canyon Brewing – exclusive rider deal on tasting paddles 🍺
• Searchlight Brewery (optional extra stop)

You’re in control of the day — ride fast, ride slow, or stop often.

What's included

Shuttle from Queenstown to Arrowtown

Flexible return options from the trail (upon availability)

eBike or pedal bike

Helmet + map + local tips

Full support (including rain rescue if needed)

What's not included

Food and drinks at cafés and breweries

Itinerary
Meet in Queenstown (Marmolada Café)
Shu

Explore Stewart Island’s remote coastline and native bush as day turns to night in search of the elusive southern brown kiwi (tokoeka).

Hunt for the Gold Rush’s greatest treasure, the mother lode of the world’s richest river!

A miner in Skippers Canyon was prospecting on Shotover River, the richest river of gold in the world. He has been found dead of natural causes after finding the largest gold nugget ever discovered in Queenstown’s history.

Before he died he claimed to have found the Shotover River’s “Mother Lode” (source of all the gold in the river) and this was where the nugget was from. So step back in time into the Gold Rush Cottage, hit the Jackpot by finding the gold nugget plus it’s origin and Escape!

Immerse yourself in the ultimate blend of adventure and cultural discovery with this extraordinary full-day combo! Begin your journey with a fully guided exploration of Mt. Tarawera, a site of immense geological and cultural significance. Infamous for its catastrophic eruption in 1886, Mt. Tarawera offers stunning volcanic landscapes and unmatched 360° panoramic views. As you trek through this dramatic terrain, your guide will share fascinating insights into the eruption’s impact on the region, including the creation of the world’s youngest geothermal valley, Waimangu. Suitable for all ages, this tour also includes the option to run into the heart of the volcanic crater for an extra adrenaline rush.

After your volcanic adventure, the cultural experience continues at Te Puia, New Zealand’s premier Māori cultural and geothermal attraction. Join the Te Pō Guided Tour, where you’ll witness the world-famous Pohutu Geyser and explore geothermal wonder

With your guide we will take you on an easy grade 40 minute walk from the Mangapurua landing following in the footsteps of the early pioneers along the old roadway to the “BRIDGE TO NOWHERE” where you will stop for lunch on this historical monument of a bygone era and learn the history from your local guide.​

The tour then includes Jet boat back down river 22kms and do an unguided 2 hours canoe or kayak (last 10 kms) back to Pipiriki stopping and exploring along the way.

Prepare for an adrenaline-pumping adventure as you snake through steep, native bush-clad canyons and tackle 14 exhilarating rapids on the iconic Kaituna River! This thrilling whitewater rafting experience includes three spectacular waterfalls, highlighted by the world-renowned 7-meter Tutea Falls—the highest commercially rafted waterfall in the world.

Whether you’re an experienced rafter or a first-timer, this unforgettable journey promises excitement for all skill levels. Blended with rich touches of Māori culture, you’ll gain deeper insights into the spiritual significance of the river and its surrounding landscapes. Your expert guides will ensure a safe and thrilling adventure, providing all the necessary river equipment to guarantee an unforgettable experience on this world-class rafting trip.

Discover the geothermal wonders of New Zealand's North Island on this full-day guided tour. Starting from Rotorua Central, traverse through lush forestry and farmland to reach the stunning Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland. Experience the spectacular eruption of the Lady Knox geyser and explore vibrant hot springs and bubbling mud pools. Continue to Waimangu Volcanic Valley, where you’ll learn about the dramatic landscape shaped by the Mt Tarawera eruption. Enjoy a scenic boat trip on Lake Rotomahana, taking in breathtaking views of steaming cliffs and geysers.

Itinerary

This itinerary is approximate and is a guide only. Variables include weather, traffic, and other road conditions.

We try to begin picking passengers up from 1:00pm. We start with our furthest away pick ups (Fernhill, for example), and then make our way back to the CBD area, out to Frankton, and then to outskirts such as Shotover Country or Jacks Point. We anticipate completing pick ups by 2pm, or slightly later on the busy day/slightly earlier on a quiet day.

We anticipate being in Lumsden around 3pm, and back to Invercargill by 4:30pm. On busy days we allow more time so we always recommend giving us a call if you're connecting to a plane or ferry so that we can be prepared to get you there on time.
We do not guarantee connections as we do not control the roads.

Activity details

Discover the extraordinary beauty of Mt Tarawera on a fully guided premium walking experience. Journey across this sacred volcanic landscape as your expert guide shares the powerful history, cultural significance, and stories of the 1886 eruption that shaped the region. Take in breathtaking 360° views from the summit of Ruawāhia and experience the thrill of descending into the heart of the volcanic crater, a truly unique experience found nowhere else in New Zealand.

After your adventure, transition from exploration to restoration at Wai Ariki Hot Springs & Spa, Rotorua’s premier cultural wellness sanctuary. Enjoy a one-hour restorative bathing experience inspired by traditional Māori healing practices, featuring geothermal mineral pools, herbal infusions, hydrotherapy, and a revitalising cold plunge overlooking Lake Rotorua. The perfect way to relax, recover, and complete an unforgettable day of culture, adventure, and wellbeing.

What's included

Fully guided walking tour led by e

Itinerary
雷穆塔卡山脉观光路线

从惠灵顿出发穿越连绵起伏的雷穆塔卡山脉，前往著名的怀拉拉帕地区，该地区以其世界级的美食生产商而闻名。途中会在海拔555米的雷穆塔卡山顶停留，欣赏令人叹为观止的360度美景。

手工芝士作坊

穿越山脉之后，您的第一站美食之旅将在一家手工芝士作坊展开。在这里，您将看到和品尝由新西兰专业奶酪师傅手工制作的各式精美奶酪产品。

精品酒庄品酒

下一站，您将前往世界级的马丁堡葡萄酒产区，该地区以黑皮诺葡萄酒闻名，您将在当地一家顶级酒庄品尝一系列精选葡萄酒，并享用精致的手工奶酪拼盘。如有需要，还可应要求安排额外的自费午餐。

手工巧克力作坊

接下来导游将带您前往精品小镇——格雷镇，在Schoc巧克力店尽情享受巧克力师穆雷·兰汉姆的精美手工巧克力。您将品尝到各种巧克力风味。

漫步 Greytown 特色小镇 （如有时间）

悠闲穿行于充满历史韵味的街道，欣赏设计师精品店、家居装饰、时尚服饰、手工首饰与独立书店，感受新西兰乡镇独有的优雅与生活美学。

Activity details

Experience the best of land and water with this thrilling full-day adventure! Start your journey with a fully guided exploration of Mt. Tarawera, infamous for its dramatic eruption in 1886. As you trek through the unique volcanic landscape, your guide will share the fascinating history of the mountain’s eruption, which created the youngest geothermal valley in the world, Waimangu, while destroying the legendary Pink and White Terraces. Take in breathtaking 360° panoramic views and enjoy the option to run into the heart of the volcanic crater—an unforgettable experience for adventurers of all ages.

After your volcanic adventure, gear up for an exciting river sledging experience on the Kaituna River. Navigate through beautiful native scenery, conquer thrilling rapids, and ride playholes—all at eye level with the river! You are in charge of your own vessel, propelled by flippers, while our expert guides provide support and encouragement along the way. No experience is necessary, making this a fun

Activity details

惠灵顿特色景点精品团半日游行程为3个小时。精品小团（2-11人），私人专车为旅行提供私密空间。资深的中文导游提供惠灵顿各个景点的详细解说。行程囊括了众多惠灵顿知名景点：从惠灵顿维多利亚山，东方湾，维塔工作室，惠灵顿缆车，植物园，布鲁克林风力发电站，国会大厦等。

What's included

专业的司机和中文导游提供路线和景点的解说

全程专车服务

我们一般使用奔驰12座的豪华旅游车。如小团的话可能会选择不同车辆

What's not included

惠灵顿缆车票（单程$6.5NZD/位，游客可以自行选择乘坐至植物园山顶，不坐缆车的游客可跟随专车去植物园山顶）

国会大厦内部的跟团游门票。（友情提醒：国会大厦内部的跟团游时间不固定，需提前几个月预订，否则不能确保有余票。）

Itinerary
维多利亚山顶观景台

从惠灵顿海滨出发，驱车上维多利亚山，到达维多利亚山顶观景台，欣赏惠灵顿市及其风景如画的海港的壮丽360度全景。

东方湾

沿着东方湾和埃文斯湾的海岸线驱车，可观赏到各种悬挂在山坡上的私人住宅使用的小缆车。

维塔工作室

简短地停留在标志性的“Wellington Blown Away”标志处，然后参观著名的维塔工商店，这是位于Miramar的维塔工作室旅游的中心。这不仅是一个零售店，它还是一个充满创意的电影人物和道具特效工作室。在维塔，您可以与等身雕像自拍，漫步于迷你博物馆，并观看独家纪录片。这里是电影爱好者的天堂。

布鲁克林风力发电站

布鲁克林风力发电机是惠灵顿标志性的山顶地标，也是新西兰首个具有商业可行性的风力发电机。

惠灵顿缆车及玫瑰园

返回市中心，短暂停留旧银行拱廊的周总拍照打卡点。可在附近选择乘坐著名的惠灵顿缆车至植物园山顶浏览市貌（票价自理），亦可漫步于诺伍德夫人玫瑰园和温室中心浏览拍照。

国会大厦、总统府建筑

参观国会大厦、总统府等建筑（不含国会大厦内部的跟团游）。

Highlights
登上维多利亚观景台，俯瞰惠灵顿港及城市全景，感受“风城”之名不是浪得虚名。
驱车沿 东方湾 及 Miramar半岛海岸线，体验惠灵顿独特的海岸风光与自然气息。
拜访维塔工

This is a great option for birthdays, parties, schools or businesses that want a real hoot, fun and games in our beautiful bay. Learn how to paddle in unison, use a board for some balancing challenges, wipe outs or a floating platform in the sunshine.

Suitable for all ages, the Big Tahuna is suitable for up to 10 children (intermediate/junior) or six to eight high school aged kids or adults.

We ask that you are comfortable in the water before hiring from us, and all equipment that you need for a great time is provided.

Please arrive 10 minutes early to kit up, sign in and be briefed.

Top Tip: For the summer months, the wind tends to pick up throughout the day, so come earlier in the day for best conditions! If the wind is too strong to be safe on the ocean, we will contact you in advance to move your booking.

Inclusions
One Mega SUP
Buoyancy Aids for all paddlers
Paddles
Instruction and Conditions Briefing

Also, check out Booking dates on the Maitai River Paddle for another awesome location for the BIG TAHUNA Party!!

Trust us, it is so much fun, aye!

Zipline into an Ancient Forest!
Tour Duration

12 hours

Tour Summary

Escape from Auckland on a private luxury tour and immerse yourself in New Zealand’s ancient forest with an unforgettable zipline adventure. Soar through the treetops, glide past towering native trees, and experience one of the country’s most pristine ecosystems, untouched for centuries. Listen to the melodic birdsong of native species as you take in the breathtaking natural beauty from a whole new perspective. After your exhilarating canopy adventure, unwind with a delicious café-style lunch before your scenic journey back.

Embark on a private canopy adventure, soaring through New Zealand’s wild forest.
Explore untouched ancient native forest and experience the country as it once was.
Traverse swing bridges, treetop platforms, a 50m high cliff walk, and an 18m controlled descent for the ultimate adrenaline rush.
Enjoy six exhilarating ziplines spanning a total of 1,200m, including a 400m tandem zipline to ride side by side.
Take in breathtaking lake and forest views while gliding up to 72 feet above the ground.

Discover the raw power of Rotorua’s geothermal landscapes on this private day tour from Auckland, uniquely combining Te Puia and Hell’s Gate. Witness bubbling mud pools, shooting geysers, and the elusive kiwi bird at Te Puia, where Māori culture comes to life through storytelling, a traditional Hāngī buffet, and an immersive cultural performance. Experience a land where myth and legend come to life, where the earth boils, and mud heals. Unwind at Hell’s Gate, home to New Zealand’s most active geothermal mud spa, where you can soak in hot steam, marvel at boiling mud pools, and indulge in a healing mud bath, known for its skin-rejuvenating properties. This one-of-a-kind journey blends nature, culture, and relaxation for an unforgettable Rotorua experience.

About

Dolphin Seafaris provide an organic experience that’s completely on the dolphin’s terms.

Experience the trip of a lifetime on the on the Bay of Plenty's premier dolphin eco-tour. On this tour, you will have the opportunity to get closer to dolphins, orcas, whales & seals on board our spacious and comfortable vessels.

You will see huge pods of common dolphins closer than you will see anywhere, playing & just doing what comes naturally as you meet them eye to eye in the clear Pacific Ocean. Our vessel Guardian is a state of the art 40ft power Catamaran which is fast, stable and has full facilities. The skipper and wildlife guides have a wealth of experienced and provide a fun and informative, family friendly tour.

Explore the outer coast of Pōhatu Marine Reserve by kayak and marvel at its spectacular towering cliffs, rock stacks, reefs, and deep-sea caves.

Flea Bay Island and the impressive island arch make an added exciting attraction in settled sea conditions. Observe the diverse wildlife and marine species such as penguins, seals, various sea birds and possibly the world’s smallest dolphin!

Best of all, no kayaking experience necessary! Trips are only run in settled, safe conditions by experienced and knowledgeable guides. Your safety is our top priority.

Scenic drive by 4WD mini vans from Akaroa

name: Otago Peninsula & Natures Wonders Eco Wildlife Tour
description:
For all ages! • 3 Hours • Spectacular Views • Remote Views

	225 Reviewers
#12 of 65 Outdoor Activities in Dunedin
TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
See rare yellow-eyed penguins, blue penguins and NZ fur seals in their breeding grounds on this 3.5hrs return tour of Natures Wonders on the Otago Peninsula.
Duration

3 Hours

About

The remote beaches at Natures Wonders are a breeding ground for remarkable wildlife. This coach tour takes you across farmland to wildlife viewing areas where you’ll see penguins, seals and cormorants in their natural habitat.
The scenery is incredible, but it’s the wildlife that steals the show on this fully-guided, small-group tour.
You’ll see:

Hoiho yellow-eyed penguins (one of the world’s rarest)
Blue penguins (the world’s smallest), and
NZ fur seals (one of the cutest).

Keep an eye skyward and you might catch a glimpse of the majestic Royal Albatross - the bird with the world’s largest wingspan.

The journey to Natures Wonders is as stunning as the destination. The drive there takes you over the high road of the Otago Peninsula to Taiaroa Head. Along the way you’ll take in the spectacular views of remote beaches, steep rolling hills, rustic farmhouses and picturesque inlets. You will also stop (photo stop) for a look at Otakou Marae before continuing to your destination.

After the tour, we will return to the city via the low road - a captivating harbour-side drive. Let Awesome Tours take you on a

The ultimate – a 90 minute guided tour telling the story of the Northern Royal Albatross and the People history of Pukekura/Taiaroa Head.
Duration

1.5 Hours

About

This 90-minute fully-guided tour includes a short film, presentation on the albatross breeding cycle and a short walk up the hill for viewing from our exclusive glassed observatory to view the albatross and then continuing through the underground fort tunnels to see the fully restored Armstrong Disappearing Gun.

Learn about our exciting history. From a small Maori camp in the 1300s to a fortified Pa where the Treaty of Waitangi was signed in 1840; from a wooden flagstaff to an 1864 lighthouse at the entrance to Otago Harbour; from an 1880s underground fort to a large defence base in WWII. Taiaroa Head is today a Nature Reserve and iconic wildlife attraction.

Things to Know

Please check in 15 minutes prior to the tour departure time to confirm your arrival.

highlights: Please check in 15 minutes prior to the tour departure time to confirm your arrival.
The Royal Albatross Centre is located at the tip of the Otago Peninsula, a 50 minute scenic drive from the Dunedin city.
Make sure you allow time to enjoy a delicious meal in the Toroa Cafe open all day.
Photography is allowed on our tours, but no flash. Viewing the albatross is behind a one way glass system to reduce our disturbance on the albatross and wildlife.
The weather at Taiaroa Head can prevail, please bring warm and waterproof clothes.

Join us on a small group day tour from Auckland to explore the Bay of Islands. Step off the beaten path and discover the authentic side of Waiheke Island on this small-group tour—crafted for those who wish to explore the island’s natural beauty, creative spirit, and artisan flavours beyond the vineyards.

Start your day with a visit a local art studio, such as the rustic workshop of John Freeman, who carves ancient swamp kauri into distinctive sculptural works, or the stunning Church Bay studio of Gabriella Lewenz, renowned for her vivid abstract oil paintings and breathtaking coastal setting.

Next explore Sacred Blessings Sanctuary Gardens, a privately owned haven featuring native trees, manicured rose gardens, organic vegetable beds, and striking Paul Dibble sculptures—an inspiring place to reconnect with nature.

Activity details

The Rangitaiki is an awesome half day Wilderness rafting trip passing through spectacular NZ scenery and Native bush.

Kaituna Cascades normally offers the Rangitaiki River Rafting Tours while the Kaituna river is flooded. Generally it’s low rocky and bouncy, but after the rains that close & flood the Kaituna river – the “Rangi” becomes and class 4/5 beast and a brown rollercoaster of fun!

The Rangitaiki is an awesome half day Wilderness rafting trip passing through spectacular NZ scenery and Native bush between the Kaiangaroa forest and the beautiful and untouched Te Urewera National Park.

The Rangi is the complete rafting package with continuous and hard whitewater at the start of the river, with larger rapids like Geoff’s Joy, Fantail Falls, Pop Up, and Tautika getting ya pumped up and holding on tight…

Then chilling out in the middle with a nice quiet flat stretch, ideal for swimming and floating followed by fun grade 3+ rapids through to the finish.

Once you’ve rafted the epic 12 km’s !! of whitewater – we’ll cruise back to Rotorua!

No prior experience is necessary as you will be given full and comprehensive training and accompanied by a highly experienced guide. All you need is a reasonable level of fitness, a sense of fun and adventure, swimwear and a to

Activity details

Pōhatu is the largest Australasian Little Penguin colony on mainland New Zealand.

This is a wonderful option for people who wish to take part in our monitoring program and learn more, in-depth, about our conservation efforts. This gives you great insight into the life of a penguin and an opportunity to see the birds up close.

First, you will set off with a walk around the colony to monitor nesting sites and make sure that our little penguins are doing well. Then, you will have the opportunity to spot some undisturbed from our hides, socializing on the water with our binoculars and our telescope, and at dusk a likelihood of some returning to shore. We depart before it gets too dark and leave it for the penguins to do their thing without us in their way.

What's included

4WD transportation from Akaroa by 4WD minibus to and from Pōhatu bay on some of Banks Peninsula back country roads,

Welcome by rare sheep breeds that can be hand-fed,

A conservation walk and talk w

Looking for an active day? Join us for a full day of adventure combining a morning hike through endemic ancient forest down our private walking track to Pohatu bay and join our sea-kayaking safari in the afternoon to encounter Pohatu marine reserve wildlife.

After being picked up in Akaroa by our booking office you will be transported by 4WD mini-van to the beginning of a 3 hours hiking trail (regular to relaxed pace). With a map and instructions from your guide you will, at your leisure, walk down part of the famous Banks Track through ancient red beech tree forest and tree ferns to Pohatu bay.

Along the way discover many waterfalls and listen to native bird songs and visit the prehistoric forest of Tutakakahikura scenic reserve with trees over 400 years old.

Once arrived at Pohatu bay you will have access to a fully equipped kitchen and bathroom facilities to relax, eat and get ready for the afternoon sea-kayaking safaris starting at 12.45pm.

Grab your land Waka (bike) from Nelson Cycling at Tahunanui Motor Camp (Tahuna Holiday Park 70 Beach Road, Tāhunanui, Nelson 7011) and after a short briefing and a 5 minute ride to the beach, you are ready to enjoy a one hour Stand Up Paddleboard (SUP) or Kayak hire from the gorgeous Tahunanui Beach and a paddle along Nelson’s iconic waterfront.

Calm and sheltered, with easy access to Fifeshire Rock and Haulashore Island, paddling at Tahuna is easy for everyone.

Following your Paddle Nelson experience, it's back on the bike to enjoy the rest of the day (3+ hours) exploring more what our awesome little city has to offer. Time cycling with spectacular views on the Great Taste Trail awaits. Follow the trail out to the local wineries and artisan breweries which are located within an easy cycling distance.

Your time is your own to enjoy a nice lunch or a tour of Pics Peanut Butter (bookings essential) before returning to the Nelson Cycling base to drop back ya wheels, or continue out to Mapua for a shuttle home. Optional upgrade for an E-bike is available too.

description:

Age

Activity details
*A full-day tour transporting you along the stunning North Otago coastline to the beautiful beach side town of Karitane. Steeped in Maori and early European history, local Maori of the area have established a cultural walking tour to the Huriawa Peninsula to showcase their historic Pā site (fortified village or stronghold) known as Te Pā a Te Wera. Your local guide will tell you about legendary Maori warriors, of fierce battles and rivalries of days gone by, while taking in the stunning views from this remarkable coastal headland.

Also included is a visit the famous Moeraki Boulders on Koekohe Beach nearby. These giant rocks situated right on the ocean shore are indeed a geological marvel, as well as having strong cultural significance for Maori. They formed millions of years ago into perfectly round spheres and have been uncovered by the breaking waves and eroding sand.

The tour will visit Dunedin City for a brief look at some of the sightseeing highlights, such as Baldwin Street (World's Steepest Street), Dunedin Railway Station and the Octagon (city center) before heading back to Port.

What's included

*Cruise ship pickup and drop off (Visitor

description:
Rotorua

5 
stars
 

192 Google reviews

See it all, the ultimate volcanic and geothermal Rotorua Tour.
Rates
Adult: NZ$200 per person
Child (age 5-15): NZ$155 per person
Under Five: Free of Charge
Times

7 Days a Week / 8am - 1 pm

Duration:

5 Hours

About

Learn about our huge colourful hot springs, erupting geysers and active boiling mud pools, spectacular volcanoes and lush native forest. All on a fully guided 1/2 day tour in Rotorua. These are small group tours no more than 15 people. Medium level of fitness required.

Visit Rotorua’s most natural and popular geothermal parks.

Wai O Tapu
Lady Knox Geyser
Waimangu Volcanic Valley
Boiling Mud Pools

The tour departs daily 8:00-8:30am and returns 12:30-1:00pm. Pick up from accommodation then drive through forestry and farmland to NZ’s largest boiling mud pool. Your guide will then show you a series of colourful hot springs and volcanic features at Wai-o-tapu. Then see the Lady Knox geyser eruption at 10.15am.

Next a short drive to the Waimangu Volcanic valley where your guide will explain the Mt Tarawera eruption which blew open this spectacular, bush covered, geothermal park, winner of NZ Eco Tourism Award 2001.

An educational experience to learn about penguins and conservation.

Come with us on a 4WD safari to the spectacular, award-winning Wild Side Conservation area! Learn about the area’s rich history, native flora and fauna and Pohatu’s unique penguin conservation program. Our Nature Safaris offer stunning views of Akaroa Harbour, the outer coast, farm land and forested hillsides. Be greeted by the farm’s, friendly pet sheep and see White-Flippered penguins as we monitor nesting sites. If you’re lucky, you may even get the chance to see some of our rehabilitation efforts such as supplemental feedings and swim therapy.

Ideal for people on a day trip in Akaroa! Gives you the chance to learn more about Pohatu Penguins while still having time to explore our historic town. Everything mentioned above is included in the 2-Hour tour.

If paddleboarding isn't your thing and you feel more comfortable on a kayak, we have got you covered! Kayaks can fare a little better in windier conditions and you can paddle a bit further in a kayak than a SUP, so come and explore with us on the water!

Hire a single sit on top sea kayak and cruise the Haven at your leisure. Suitable for kids, right up to full-grown adult kids, we suggest children are accompanied with a strong paddler on each kayak.

Following a briefing on the conditions and any tips you need for your kayaking, you are free to enjoy the bay lapping up the sun or you can paddle out to Fifeshire Rock or Haulashore Island and enjoy the best that Tahunanui has to offer, from the water.

Top Tip: For the summer months, the wind tends to pick up throughout the day, so come earlier in the day for best conditions! If the wind is too strong to be safe on the ocean, we will contact you in advance to move your booking.

Enjoy a full day of guided, private scenic adventure that includes a river-trail walk, bike ride and a guided twilight glow worm kayak.
About
The day starts at our Riverside Adventures base where we will fit you with your bikes before hopping aboard our comfortable shuttle van to Jones Landing, where your adventure begins.

You will enjoy an easy hour-long walk along the Waikato River trail where you will experience views of nearby Lake Arapuni and beyond.

We will meet you at the end of the trail with your bikes. The 2km ride begins at the Arapuni Dam and continues along the Waikato River Trail to village of Arapuni where you will stop for lunch at the popular Rhubarb Cafe.

Following lunch, your bike ride continues for 12km, returning you back to our base where you can pause before the day’s final adventure.

Next, your guide will help you get kitted out and ready for the Twilight Kayak Glow Worm Tour.

Over the next three hours bask in the magic of the Pokaiwhenua canyon as you hear stories from your experienced guide, a few tasty treats and, finally, an experience like no other as you float silently back through the canyon surrounded by twinkling glow worms, stars above

Nature and wildlife viewing opportunities
Sometimes, earthquakes in or near Fiordland are felt at Piopiotahi Milford Sound. They might shake objects on a shelf or rattle a window, but they do not cause major impacts or disrupt your visit.
Large earthquakes are rare, and extremely unlikely to happen during a short visit. But they will happen again.
DROP, COVER and HOLD until shaking stops. Most injuries from earthquakes happen when you try and move during the shaking. The lower to the ground/floor you are, the less likely you are to be injured.
Follow staff instructions and/or move up and away from waterways (the fiord and rivers) as quickly as possible.

The tour commences with a drive along the scenic northern route, taking in spectacular views of the Hauraki Gulf and beyond to the Coromandel Peninsula.

Visit three vineyards for wine tasting with a delicious vineyard platter-style lunch at the second vineyard, including a glass of wine.

If time allows, after lunch head towards Onetangi, a 1.87-kilometre long, north-facing beach with beautiful old Pohutukawa trees on the foreshore. For many years it has been the site of the Onetangi Beach Races.

The final stop is at a picturesque vineyard overlooking the Hauraki Gulf. Enjoy a wine tasting here and take in the view.

If you wish, from here you can stay on for dinner (at your own expense) and make your own way back to the ferry when you are ready or alternatively the guide will return you to the Waiheke Wharf in time to catch the 5pm ferry back to Auckland.

Enjoy a guided bike tour along the Cambridge to Lake Karapiro section of the Te Awa River Ride.

Our guides let you know what's coming up, and share their knowledge and history of the area as you soak in the surroundings of extremely popular section of trail, which takes you from through the town of Town of Trees and Champions out to the famous Lake Karapiro.

A 30 minute guided tour of underground Fort Taiaroa, built on the headland to counter the potential threat of a Russian invasion in the late 1880s and later used for training and defence during WWI and WWII. See the only fully restored 1889 Armstrong Disappearing Gun in the world in its original position.
Duration

30 minutes

About

Fort Taiaroa, established over 100 years ago to counter the threat of invasion from Tsarist Russia, is hidden beneath the albatross colony. Visitors can explore this fascinating secret fort, learn the military history, from Maori pa to WW2 Fort and view the only Armstrong Disappearing Gun of its type in the world, still in working trim, in its original gun pit. It’s a must-do tour to gain a full appreciation of this precious historic and wildlife site – you’ll be engrossed by the interesting stories of life during the military eras.

Things to Know

Please check in 15 minutes prior to the tour departure time to confirm your arrival.

highlights: Please check in 15 minutes prior to the tour departure time to confirm your arrival.

After you finish your guided 2.5-hour Hobbit trip, we start our way to Rotorua, where we visit Te Puia.

After approximately two hours, we head back to Auckland CBD.

Enjoy the Waitomo Glowworm Cave Tour with Auckland & Beyond Tours. New Zealand has a unique species of tiny glowing creatures called “Arachnocampa Luminoso.” Thousands of these magical tiny living lights radiate their luminescent light all around the Waitomo Grotto. The tour starts by picking you up from Auckland CBD. Then, we go on a two-and-a-half-hour scenic drive in South Auckland and into the Waikato region, with a scenic stop for amazing photos and any other stops if needed. On arrival, you will have around 30 minutes for some shopping in the Gift Shop and grabbing a drink or something to eat at the Waitomo Homestead. Your Guide will take you on a 45-minute tour inside the Waitomo Glowworm Cave, explaining the history of the Cave, the glowing worms, and the formations you’ll see inside. Afterward, you’ll ride on a flat-bottom boat that will glide through the Waitomo river while you gaze silently at the sparkling worms. After you finish your Glowworm Experience, we will make our way back through the scenic roads to Auckland CBD.

What's included

The tour starts by picking you up from Auckland CBD. Then, we go on a two-and-a-half-hour scenic drive in South Auckland and into the Waikato region, with a scenic stop for amazing photos and any other stops if needed. On arrival, you will have around 30 minutes for some shopping in the Gift Shop and grabbing a drink or something to eat at the Waitomo Homestead. Your Guide will take you on a 45-minute tour inside the Waitomo Glowworm Cave, explaining the history of the Cave, the glowing worms, and the formations you’ll see inside. Afterward, you’ll ride on a flat-bottom boat that will glide through the Waitomo river while you gaze silently at the sparkling worms. After you finish your Glowworm Experience, we will make our way back through the scenic roads to Rotorua CBD.

Visit one of New Zealand’s Geothermal wonders situated in the Whakarewarewa on a half day tour from Auckland City. Te Puia Geothermal Valley Rotorua is an active geothermal wonderland!

This 60-hectare site is home to the New Zealand Maori Arts and Crafts Institute, alive kiwi enclosure, the world-famous Pohutu Geyser, and more than 500 natural geothermal wonders.

Tour starts with us picking you up from Auckland CBD; then you enjoy a three-hour scenic drive towards the Bay of Plenty.

On arrival, you will be welcomed by the Māori residents and offered to choose your steam box meal (extra cost) to be cooked in Woven baskets and placed in the active steam vent.

After that; your tour guide will take you through a 60 mins tour around 60 hectares of Te Puia Geothermal Valley including the largest active geyser in the Southern Hemisphere, the Pohutu Geyser.

You can also See the native Kiwi bird, Mud Pools, watch New Zealand’s iconic native Carvings and Weaving performed in front of you, as well as Learning about the Māori Culture while performing at the sacred Marae.

Our Twilight at The OPERA tour provides a unique opportunity to experience our private eco-reserve at sunset.

This tour offers a unique opportunity to view our

, hoiho (yellow-eyed penguins) and a variety of our marine and coastal species, in their natural habitat.

This 90-minute guided tour consists of three parts.

Begin your experience in a theatre setting, where you will learn about our conservation work and the hoiho (yellow-eyed penguin) and efforts to protect them.

Then move into our rehabilitation centre where hoiho (yellow-eyed penguins) and other penguin species can be seen up close (when birds are in our care).

Finally, a short bus trip into our reserve where you are guided on an approximately 2km (1.25 mile) nature walk with the potential to see numerous plants and animals, including yellow-eyed penguins and little penguins, fur seals, sea lions, and several species of shore and land birds, all of which reside in the reserve.

Then take a short bus trip into our res

Trail Route
The Pou at the National Park Village Park and Ride indicates the start of your journey. Following the Fishers Track marked signs towards the right and then left and over the railway line. It is then straight up a short steady climb up the Fishers/Kurua gravel road. The first section of the trail takes you through Erua forest.

Ecologically and geographically important, it is home to a significant fault line which contributes to the ranges and wetlands. The area is also home to unique and rare orchids, populations of Kiwi and regenerating bush.

从惠灵顿出发，沿着赫特山谷顺着赫特河前行，途中穿越壮丽的雷穆塔卡山脉，来到费瑟斯顿手工芝士作坊，品尝多款当地手工制作的奶酪。随后前往享誉盛名的马丁堡葡萄酒产区，参观三家顶级酒庄。在经验丰富的品酒师引导下，品鉴各家酒庄的代表佳酿。这趟旅程完美融合了壮丽的自然风光、屡获殊荣的美酒与纯正的地方风味，是逃离喧嚣的理想半日之旅。

带您前往新西兰顶级葡萄酒产区之一——马丁堡。参观马丁堡三家顶级酒庄，于酒窖中品尝精选葡萄酒，领略其独特风味。旅程完美融合了壮丽的自然风光、屡获殊荣的美酒与纯正的地方风味，是逃离喧嚣的理想半日之旅。

What's not included

Itinerary
雷穆塔卡山脉观光路线

从惠灵顿出发穿越连绵起伏的雷穆塔卡山脉，前往著名的怀拉拉帕地区，该地区以其世界级的美食生产商而闻名。途中会在海拔555米的雷穆塔卡山顶停留，欣赏令人叹为观止的360度美景。

手工芝士作坊

穿越山脉之后，您的第一站美食之旅将在一家手工芝士作坊展开。在这里，您将看到和品尝由新西兰专业奶酪师傅手工制作的各式精美奶酪产品。

精品酒庄品酒

参观马丁堡三家顶级精品酒庄，带您了解该地区的葡萄酒知识，品尝精选葡萄酒，领略其独特风味。酒庄的选择不固定，受季节和时段的影响。酒庄午餐可根据游客要求提供，游客自费。

返程

行程最后，您将通过雷穆塔卡山脉返回惠灵顿市中心。

Highlights
驱车穿越壮丽的雷穆塔卡山脉，一路进入美食与美酒并存的怀拉拉帕地区。
在手工芝士作坊品尝新西兰手工奶酪，了解当地乳制品匠艺。
探访马丁堡世界级葡萄酒产区，品鉴庄园的特色葡萄酒，包括享誉盛名的黑皮诺。

Highlights of all the best spots in Auckland
Tour Duration

8 hours

Tour Summary

Join us for a unique, fun, and informative small group city tour of Auckland, New Zealand’s largest city! With an exceptional value, our comprehensive tour covers all the best sights with plenty of stops for photos. Discover our sparkling harbour, view the iconic Harbour Bridge, cruise through to Tamaki Drive, stunning beaches, historic buildings and admire panoramic views from the summit of our highest volcano, Mt Eden. Walk through the sacred native rainforest, the rugged coastline and windswept, black-sand beaches. Home to unique wildlife and towering ancient trees, and some of the most breath-taking views you’ll ever see! The ‘wild west’ feels like another world away. Experience the untamed beauty and indulge in breath-taking panoramic viewpoints over the Tasman Sea, which separates New Zealand from Australia.

Tour Highlights

Discover Auckland's highlights including the wild west coast native forest and black sand beach.

Take in great views of the Hauraki Gulf, the Harbour Bridge, spectacular ocean vistas and the Rangitoto Island.

Drive past Westhaven Marina, one of the largest marinas in the Southern Hemisphere.

Cruise through the city to Tamaki Drive, Auckland D

Experience the film set of The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings fame!

The Hobbiton Movie Set Tour is where Peter Jackson filmed his famous Lord of the Rings and The Hobbit film trilogies.

The Hobbiton Movie Set is one of the most visited destinations in New Zealand for the past 10 years.

Tour starts by picking you up from Auckland CBD, then you’ll enjoy a two-hour scenic drive South of Auckland into Waikato, with a scenic stop for amazing photos and any other stops if needed.

Once we arrive, you spend 30–45 mins shopping in the official Hobbiton Gift Shop and grabbing a drink or a bite in the famous Shire’s Rest Café.

Then your middle earth Journey starts by hopping on The Hobbiton Bus that takes you to The Hobbit Village where your guide will do a two-hour guided tour through The Hobbit Holes, The Mill, The Double Arched Bridge and have a complimentary drink at The Green Dragon Inn.

Be prepared to take lots of photos and videos in this unique place.

After you finish your amazing Hobbit tour, we make our way through the scenic roads of Waikato back to Auckland CBD.

After we Pick you up from Auckland CBD, We start our journey south of Auckland through a breathtaking two and a half hours scenic drive into The Waikato Region towards Waitomo Glow Worm Caves.

Once we arrive, you will have a chance to get a snack or warm drink at the cafe. Then you’ll be escorted by The Waitomo Caves tour guide to start your tour inside Waitomo Grotto.

Inside the cave, you’ll be Walking through the marvelous natural light display created by the tiny living worms that are unique to New Zealand while listening to your tour guide explain the history and significance of the Grotto.

Then you’ll hop on board a flat bottom boat that will take you through the Waitomo River where you will get really close to the fascinating glowing creatures.

After that; you’ll Jump back on our luxurious vehicle to start our two-hour drive to Rotorua.

Te Puia is the best spot to experience NZ Geothermal Wonders, Māori Culture, Native birds and plants. Over there you will also watch Pohutu Geyser erupt, which is the Larges

We start by picking you up from Auckland CBD, then we head to Mission Bay waterfront suburb about 5 km east of Auckland CBD. Driving through the scenic Tamaki Drive with unobstructed views of Rangitoto Island

After that, we take you to MJ Savage Memorial Park, known as Bastion Point. Which overlooks the Harbour Bridge, Waitemata Harbour, and Auckland City Skyline.

We will also take you to Achilles Point, where you will capture some amazing photos with Rangitoto Island as your background; Rangitoto Island is the most recent and the largest Volcano in the Hauraki Gulf area.

One Tree Hill “Maungakiekie” is another volcanic summit in Auckland City, sets at 182-metre (597 ft) & the second-largest volcanic field intact in Auckland City. You will enjoy the 360 view of Auckland at One Tree hill summit and learn about the history of the mount.

Our Next Stop will be at Mount Eden Summit. One of the most prominent volcanic cones remaining in the Auckland region, where you’ll be able to see amazing views over the Hauraki Gulf.

Now our tour comes to an end, We drive back to Auckland CBD for your hotel drop off.

Activity details
We bring you the ultimate cultural experience!

Enter the world of Mitai, and learn about the history of this area which was once flourished in villages.

Cultural Performance & Experience!

Our stories unfold through the dynamic scenes of warriors paddling the war canoe down Wai-o-Whiro stream. The melodious sounds of instruments and entertainment are highlighted through the haka and performing arts. Enjoy the traditional Hangi dinner followed by an evening walk through the forest visiting the old village site and discovering the sacred natural spring Te Puna Tapu a Tuhoe. End the evening in awe of the glow worms lighting the banks of the forest.

Buffet, the delicious tempting traditional hangi food of the Great Ocean of Kiwa!

Our hāngī earth oven kai (food) includes meat, chicken, kumara and much more.

The hāngī is cooked in an earth-oven, a hole 1-2m into the ground, the baskets of kai are placed on hot stones at the bottom of a hāngī pit. The kai is then covered to trap the heat, the steam and heat from heated hāngī stones infuses the kai with delicious earthy flavours while it cooks for 3-4 hours. Come and try out the delicious tempting food.

name: Otago Peninsula & The Royal Albatross tour
description:
For all ages! • 3 Hours • Knowledgable Experience • Highly Educational

	225 Reviewers

TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
An absolutely marvellous tour with a great tour guide.
Duration

3 Hours

About

Take this fantastic opportunity to visit the Royal Albatross Centre at Taiaroa Head. This breath-taking location on the Otago Peninsula is home to the only mainland Northern Royal Albatross breeding colony in the world.

Watch the albatross come and go overhead, gliding on the wind or nesting on the hillside. With its 3-metre wingspan, the Northern Royal Albatross is quite a sight to see.
See it with Awesome Tours.

The 1-hour, fully guided tour includes:

A short film
Presentation on the Northern Royal Albatross breeding cycle
A short walk up the hill to a viewing observatory
Explore the centre and its surrounds, with impressive views, a delightful gift shop and cafe, and everything you ever wanted to know about albatross.

The journey to the Royal Albatross Colony is as stunning as the destination. The drive there takes you over the high road of the Otago Peninsula to Taiaroa Head. Along the way you will take in the spectacular views of remote beaches, steep rolling hills, rustic farmhouses, and picturesque inlets. You will also stop for a look at Otakou Marae before continuing to your destination.

After the tour, we will return to the city via the low road - a captivating harbour-side driv

What's included

奥塔里-威尔顿布什本土植物园

毛皮海豹海滩

穿越私人农场

提供茶、咖啡与小点心

Itinerary

停留点：奥塔里-威尔顿布什本土植物园 （停留时间：30分钟）
地址：160 Wilton Road, Wellington 6012, New Zealand

我们从奥塔里-威尔顿布什开始旅程——这里拥有一百公顷的古老与再生森林，是新西兰唯一专门种植本土植物的植物园。该植物园被新西兰皇家园艺学会旗下的新西兰园林信托评为“六星国际重要性园林”。在这里，我们将向您介绍早期毛利人和欧洲定居者眼中的新西兰景观。漫步在空中栈道上，您将欣赏树冠层的美景，同时由友善且知识渊博的导游介绍一些新西兰特有的独特动植物，以及它们为生存所面临的挑战。我们经常能看到美丽的本地鸟类，如Tui、Kaka、Kakariki、Kereru，有时还能见到Karearea——这些鸟类在25年前在惠灵顿地区曾一度濒临灭绝或已经灭绝。

停留点：特拉惠提牧场 （停留时间：1小时30分钟）
地址：Terawhiti Station, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand

About

If paddleboarding isn't your thing and you feel more comfortable on a kayak, we have got you covered! Kayaks can fare a little better in windier conditions and you can paddle a bit further in a kayak than a SUP, so come and explore with us on the water!

Hire a single sit on top sea kayak and cruise the Haven at your leisure. Suitable for kids from approx 8yrs old, right up to full-grown adult kids, our kayaks are stable and fun!

Following a briefing on the conditions and any tips you need for your kayaking, you are free to enjoy the bay lapping up the sun or you can paddle out to Fifeshire Rock or Haulashore Island and enjoy the best that Tahunanui has to offer, from the water.

Top Tip: For the summer months, the wind tends to pick up throughout the day, so come earlier in the day for best conditions! If the wind is too strong to be safe on the ocean, we will contact you in advance to move your booking.

Please arrive 10 minutes early to kit up, sign in and be briefed.

We ask that you are comfortable in the water before hiring

Activity details

Piha is New Zealand’s most famous surf beach, situated on the west coast of the North Island. This black iron-sand beach has a reputation for awesome surfs that roll in over the Tasman Sea. See this and more on our West Coast Discovery!

The tour starts with picking you up from Auckland CBD; then, you’ll enjoy a 30-minute scenic drive through the Waitakere Ranges. The first stop is the Arataki Visitor Centre, where you will learn all about New Zealand’s Native trees and look out to panoramic views of the Waitakere Ranges – the perfect start to exploring the west coast. We then take a 20-minute drive to Kitekite waterfalls.

Once we arrive at Kitekite waterfalls, you will enjoy a 5-minute easy stroll to the waterfall- a great spot for photos! We then head to the famous Piha Surf Beach, where you can go for a swim or just relax and enjoy the stunning views.

You can also walk up the Lion Rock (situated in the middle of the beach) for stunning views and an amazing photo opportunity. ​

After that, we head back to Auckland CBD.

What's included

Pick up From Auckland CBD

Piha Beach & Lion Rock

Kitekite Waterfall

Arataki Mao

Step aboard Tiua, Taupō Sailing Adventures’ luxurious sailing catamaran, for a relaxing and scenic afternoon on the water. As the lake settles into the warmth of midday, enjoy a smooth and peaceful cruise toward the iconic Māori Rock Carvings—an awe-inspiring 14-metre artwork carved into the cliffs and only accessible by boat.

This eco-friendly sailing experience is powered by the wind, offering a quiet and gentle way to connect with the natural beauty of Lake Taupō. With her stable twin-hull design, Tiua provides a smooth ride and plenty of space to stretch out and enjoy the journey. Her spacious saloon, covered cockpit, and open deck areas are designed to keep you comfortable in all weather.

65 minutes

About

This is it! – if you have dreamt of truly experiencing the ultimate helicopter tour of the Aoraki/Mt Cook National Park, then this signature helicopter flight will tick every box.

The ultimate way to view the awe-inspiring spectacle that is Aoraki/Mt Cook.
First, the helicopter will circle New Zealand’s highest peak which is a truly mesmerising experience.
After, fly over the famous Fox and Franz Josef glaciers, and witness the wild ocean coastline of the West Coast.
In addition, this signature helicopter tour includes a landing on the Haupapa/Tasman Glacier.

You will leave with memories and photos to last a lifetime alongside a newfound love and appreciation for New Zealand’s natural beauty.

Once airborne again, we transfer you and your bike across the Tasman River to Black Point, the starting point of your cycle journey.

About

Immerse yourself in the enchanting beauty of Lake Matheson with Glacier Valley Eco Tours' Lake Matheson Nature Tour. Nestled in an ancient forest, Lake Matheson is renowned for its stunning mirror views of Aoraki/Mount Cook and Mount Tasman.

Departing at the Glacier Valley Eco Tours base in the township of Franz Josef Glacier - we’ll drive you to Fox Glacier from Franz Josef Glacier (a 30-minute drive) or if you are staying in Fox Glacier we'll pick you up from our meeting point in the Fox Glacier township (or your accommodation).

​

This Great Short Walk gently meanders and undulates around the lake on a well-formed track, crossing picturesque bridges and boardwalks over vibrant mountain streams, making it suitable for all ages and abilities.

Along the way, our knowledgeable guides will share fascinating insights into the history of the area, the unique conditions that create the lake's spectacular mirror reflections, and much more.

For the best chance to witness these iconic reflections, join us in the early evening.

Experience the magic of Lake Matheson and let its serene beauty captivate you.

About

Embark on the Ōkārito Nature Tour with Glacier Valley Eco Tours and explore a hidden gem of New Zealand's South Westland.

Just a 20-minute drive from Franz Josef Glacier, the small coastal village of Ōkārito offers an unparalleled bird-watching experience amidst stunning natural diversity. Suitable for all ages and abilities, this tour includes a shuttle service from the Franz Josef township or guests can meet us in Okarito. The tour guides you through lush rainforests, estuaries, sea cliffs and wetlands, revealing breathtaking views of the Southern Alps, including Aoraki/Mt Cook, Mt Tasman and even Franz Josef Glacier on clear days. Delve into the rich history of gold mining and early settlement with the opportunity to encounter the area's rich birdlife - up to 76 bird species, such as the famous white heron (Kotuku), fern birds, kakariki and kea.

Visit the Ōkārito Community Native Plant Nursery to plant a native tree as part of our Okarito Restoration project. The journey to this magical place begins in spectacular style on this luxury private tour from Auckland to Hokianga, Northland and the Bay of Islands. Explore some of the many locations of local and cultural significance including Tane Mahuta, the world-famous and largest Kauri Tree in New Zealand, Hokianga harbour and Waitangi, the ‘Birthplace of our Nation’. These regions are wildly beautiful, charmingly rustic and steeped in fascinating history. With its ancient forests, unique cultural experiences, breathtaking landscapes, and awesome adventure opportunities, Northland is a paradise for heritage explorers.

Set off from Auckland on a 3-day luxury private tour through New Zealand’s North Island, beginning with the vibrant geothermal pools of Wai-O-Tapu and the powerful Huka Falls. Continue to Napier, renowned for its striking 1930s Art Deco architecture and coastal elegance. Explore Cape Kidnappers on a 4WD safari to one of the world’s largest mainland gannet colonies, perched above dramatic ocean cliffs. On your return journey, unwind with a scenic Lake Taupō cruise to the iconic Māori Rock Carvings. With curated experiences, a private driver-guide, and 4-star accommodation, this journey offers the perfect blend of nature, culture, and comfort.

​Itinerary At a Glance

Day 1: Discover Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland, Huka falls and Travel to Napier. Overnight Stay in Napier.

Day 2: Cape Kidnappers Gannet Safari Tour & Art Deco Walking City Tour. Overnight Stay in Napier.

Day 3: Lake Taupo Waterfront, Māori Rock Carvin

Day 2: Cape Kidnappers Gannet Safari Tour & Art Deco Walking City Tour. Overnight Stay in Napier.
Day 3: Lake Taupo Waterfront, Māori Rock Carvings Scenic Cruise, Huka Honey Hive and return to Auckland.

Discover serenity in the most delicate and intimate way. Get the most out of your visit to Rotorua with a package to explore more of an unforgettable experience. The city’s unique location means that it’s perched on the Pacific Rim of Fire, making the city and the experience, a classic combination of geothermal activity and vibrant Māori culture. A tour of Te Puia Geothermal Valley is the best way to experience this top visitor attraction in New Zealand. Feel the rush of adrenaline and enjoy an action-packed jet boating around Lake Rotorua, along with a dip in the hot geothermal pools of Deluxe Lake Spa, a geothermal bathing and soul soothing retreat, and explore the world’s youngest geothermal valley at your own pace at Waimangu Volcanic Valley.

About

Enjoy a scenic journey from Port Chalmers as you explore the vibrant city of Dunedin and the stunning Otago Peninsula – known as the wildlife capital of New Zealand.

Begin with a stop at one of the most photographed buildings in the Southern Hemisphere – the iconic Dunedin Railway Station. Then, get your camera ready as we wind our way along the Otago Peninsula, taking in breathtaking views of the harbour, bays, beaches, and an array of wading birds.

The highlight of your experience is a 1.5-hour guided tour at Penguin Place, a private conservation reserve dedicated to protecting the endangered Yellow-Eyed Penguin. During the tour, you may have the chance to see these rare penguins, along with other native wildlife such as New Zealand fur seals, sea lions, and occasionally little blue penguins in their nesting sites.

Discover the magnificent Okarito Lagoon
Duration

5 hours

About

Did you know?
Okarito lagoon is New Zealand’s largest unmodified wetland area - surrounded by national park and rainforest, home to a variety of wading and forest birds and takes a special place in the history books of the West Coast.

Join Paula & Swade onboard the 'Explorer Douglas'. During your 2 hour EcoTour they engage in both an informative and friendly way to help you discover the absolute magic of Okarito and its stunning environment - from wetlands to rainforest and mountain landscapes, and an amazing diverse range of bird life.
Then our local guide will meet you off the boat at the wharf us for a 3 hour guided walk. With mountain and glacier views, flora & fauna, lagoon, beach, rich history, amazing sunrises & the best bird song in the area, this is one of New Zealand's hidden gems.

What to Bring

Outdoor clothing for the day’s weather conditions, spare warm top layer of clothing, comfortable walking shoes, sun hat, sunblock, lip balm, sunglasses, camera & water bottle.

2 hours guided tour on the “Explorer Douglas” - customised for the Okarito lagoon and wetlands
cup of tea and home baked treat on board

About

The Great War (WWI) is the stage on which the story of aviation and the ‘Knights of the Sky’ comes to life. This is the personal collection of film director Sir Peter Jackson and through his generosity a series of dioramas, created by WingNut Films and enhanced by lifelike mannequins by Wētā Workshop combined to provide a museum experience, never seen before its opening in 2006.

Rare memorabilia on display; the envy of any national collection ranges from beautifully crafted ‘trench’ art through to personal items belonging to national flying heroes such as the USA’s Eddie Rickenbacker, France’s René Fonck and Germany’s Red Baron, Manfred von Richthofen and the infamous Hermann Goering.

No aviation museum would be complete without flying machines and ‘Knights of the Sky’ has a mixture of original, flyable, and static replica aircraft. The rarity and beauty of these machines including a Curtiss MF Flying Boat, the Italian time capsule Capro

About

The Dangerous Skies exhibition is not just another World War Two showcase. It's a journey into the lesser-known stories of the war on the Eastern front, featuring the world’s only female fighter aces and the most famous women regiments: Russia’s 588th Night Bombers, or as the Germans called them, ‘Night Witches’. Prepare to be surprised and intrigued!

Like World War One’s Knights of the Sky, Dangerous Skies features mannequins made by Wētā Workshop and original, flyable static aircraft in larger-than-life dioramas. Opened in 2016, Dangerous Skies has undergone several changes and is now the home to nine WW2 aircraft.

The most recent additions are an original Mosquito and a P-40 Kitty Hawk, along with a Messerschmitt Bf108, once owned and flown by German ace Franz Stigler and a Lockheed Hudson, an American-built light bomber and coastal reconnaissance aircraft that has been suspended in a dramatic crash scene in the depths o

The Big Backcountry Ride
Full Day Bike Ride

We take you on a drive for about 55 minutes to a high point on the Napier – Taihape Road, the famous Gentle Annie. From here, you can enjoy 40kms riding back to River Valley Lodge. This is a leisurely ride, with several very long downhills and a couple of significant uphills. This ride starts in the high country, with spectacular views of Mt. Ruapehu and the Otupae and Ruahine Ranges.

The route takes you through Otupae and Mangaohane Stations, and then over the Rangitikei River at the Mangaohane Bridge, where, if it is a hot day, you may choose to go for a swim.

Our E-Bikes

We have a range of e-bike sizes, from XS-XL, and two frame types, either step through or a more mountain bike style. The Liv Tempt bikes are primarily designed for women, whereas the Giant Talon bikes are unisex.

Emerson’s was first brewed in August of 1985, producing a meagre four and a half liters on a stove in a kitchen by a young, excitable Richard Emerson. Since that moment Emerson’s has grown to one of New Zealand’s biggest craft brewerie

This is morning on the lake as it should be—quiet, spacious, and effortlessly enjoyable. A relaxed and luxurious way to begin your day in one of New Zealand’s most beautiful places.

Activity details

Start your morning with a serene and unforgettable sail across Lake Taupō aboard Tiua, Taupō Sailing Adventures’ luxury sailing catamaran.
As the hustle and bustle of the day begins, you can lean back and enjoy the cruise toward the remarkable Māori Rock Carvings—an iconic 14-metre masterpiece carved into the cliffs and only accessible by water.

Sailing aboard Tiua offers a unique blend of comfort and connection with nature. Powered by the wind, this eco-friendly experience is smooth, quiet, and relaxing. The catamaran’s stable twin-hull design ensures a gentle ride, while her spacious layout offers comfort in all conditions. A roomy indoor saloon, covered cockpit, and open deck areas mean you can enjoy the journey in sun or shelter, with both indoor and outdoor seating availa
highlights: Tea or Coffee and a Biscuit

Arrive at the Iconic Māori Rock Carvings

Hear Stories from the Crew
Learn about the history of the carvings and the lake itself from our friendly and knowledgeable crew as you sail along.

Take a Refreshing Swim (weather dependent)
If conditions allow, enjoy a swim in the clear, volcanic waters of Lake Taupō—a rejuvenating way to start your day.

Relax on the Return Sail
Stretch out and unwind in the spacious indoor or outdoor seating areas as we gently sail back to the marina.

A Spectacular rediscovery of the historic Pink & White Terraces, an Eighth Wonder of the World!
Tour Duration

13 hours

Tour Summary

Prepare for a day of wonder, fascination, and discovery as you immerse yourself in the unique environment of Waimangu Volcanic Valley on this luxurious private day tour from Auckland. Explore the world’s youngest geothermal valley on scenic walking trails surrounded by steaming landscapes, rare native birdlife, and vibrant volcanic features. Then cruise across Lake Rotomahana, the final resting place of the lost Pink and White Terraces—once known as the eighth wonder of the world. This unforgettable journey blends geothermal beauty, natural history, and serene lake views in one extraordinary experience.

Tour Highlights
Enjoy a scenic drive through lush countryside with opportunity for photo stops.
Witness the world’s youngest geothermal valley, its unique history and legacy.
Breathtaking views over crater lakes, silica terraces, and the volcanic valley.
Boat cruise to see geothermal wonders and the sites of the lost Pink & White Terraces.
See Frying Pan Lake, the world’s largest hot water spring.

Discover an ancient world 30 million years in the making and marvel at Mother Nature's light display
Tour Duration

11 hours

Tour Summary

Escape the city and journey into the heart of New Zealand’s underground wonders on a premium private tour from Auckland to Waitomo. Marvel at the mesmerizing glow of the native Arachnocampa Luminosa as you glide silently through the world-famous Waitomo Glowworm Caves, then continue your adventure with a guided walking tour of Ruakuri Cave, where ancient limestone formations and hidden waterfalls create a breathtaking spectacle. Enjoy seamless travel in a luxury private vehicle with a knowledgeable driver guide, and savor a relaxing café-style lunch before your return to Auckland, making this an unforgettable full-day experience.

Tour Highlights
Marvel at stunning stalactites, stalagmites, and limestone formations in the iconic Glowworm Caves.
Glide silently by boat beneath thousands of mesmerizing glowworms lighting up the darkness.
Walk through the Cathedral Cave and experience its breathtaking acoustics.
Discover the longest underground walking tour in Waitomo as you explore Ruakuri Cave.

At Wānaka Lavender Farm, we're delighted to welcome photographers, amateurs and professionals alike, to capture the mesmerising beauty of our lavender farm.

Photography Fee for Professional Shoots

Professional photographers, filmmakers, and those conducting shoots for commercial, promotional, advertising, or resale purposes are required to book their sessions in advance and pay a photography fee.

Determining Professional Shoots

We consider special occasion photos (e.g. engagement and wedding photography), family portraits, landscape, library photography, and any other commercial photography to be professional. If you're unsure if your shoot falls into this category, please reach out to us.

Commercial Photography/Filming

Catalogue shoots, shoots requiring props, additional lighting or multiple costumes, commercials, professional videos and the like are subject to certain terms, restrictions, and fees based upon the nature of the production request. Please contact us for more information.

Explore the Otago Harbour and beyond…
By the time you return to your ship you’ll understand why Dunedin is the Wildlife Capital of NZ!
Duration:

2.5 Hours

About:

We’ll pick you up from the Port Chalmers Cruise Terminal and shuttle you 5-minutes to our boat, the Vivienne J. Once aboard the boat your expert crew will take you on a Wildlife Cruise around the Otago Harbour and beyond.

The small-group cruise will travel around beautiful wharves and boatsheds, then through the historic Quarantine Islands. You’ll see the Careys Bay fishing village and its motley collection of boats before continuing along the harbour. Enjoy a nice hot drink and biscuit for morning tea.

As you arrive at the entrance to the Otago Harbour you’ll see the tidal Aramoana sandflats and the spectacular volcanic cliffs of Taiaroa Head. You’ll see the royal albatross breeding colony, the only mainland colony in the world. Beneath a picturesque lighthouse you’ll find a large population of fur seals, watch as they swim, play and sleep on rocky platforms close to the boat. A short way out to sea we often spot other species of albatross and seabirds. We sometimes see dolphins, sea lions and blue pen

Small-group tour of Larnach Castle, a bus journey across the Otago Peninsula, followed by a special Wildlife Cruise!
Duration:

5 - 6 Hours

About

We’ll pick you up from the Port Chalmers Cruise Terminal. You’ll be greeted by one of our friendly staff holding a sign with your name printed clearly on it. From there you’ll bus along the western side of the Otago Harbour to Dunedin.

Your bus journey will take you up over the Otago Peninsula High Road to Larnach Castle. Along the way you’ll be treated to magnificent views of the rugged coastline and beautiful harbour.

Your guide will then take you on a personalised tour of Larnach Castle. Built in 1871, it took two hundred workers roughly three years to build the exterior shell and a further twelve years for master European craftsmen to finish the interior. Over the years the castle and grounds fell in to ruin and disrepair. In 1967 the property was bought by the Barker family who have spent many years restoring it to its former grandeur. The rooms are once again filled with original artworks, clothing and furniture.

About

Frequently referred to as one of New Zealand's most spectacular destinations, Omanawa Falls is situated in the stunning Kaimai ranges. At its heart featuring a breathtaking 35m waterfall cascading into a pristine pool surrounded by lush New Zealand native rainforest. Kaitiaki Adventures will lead you on an unparalleled, culturally integral, and historic guided walk that immerses you in the rich cultural history of the Ngati Hangarau people. A regenerative tourism experience in the unique cultural space of Omanawa Falls.

Duration

2.5 Hours

More

Join our friendly, knowledgeable local guides through the beautiful trails to 2 lookouts, the Māori carvings, the century old hydro electric power station service tunnel and the breathtaking Omanawa falls. Departing Omanawa falls follows a visit to McLaren Falls and Tauranga's best view point the Minden Lookout.

Immerse yourself in the surrounding flora and fauna while listening to the stories of the area, the Awa (River), and the cultural values of mana whenua.
