Join Us on a Cultural Tour of New Zealand’s most significant Maaori earthwork fortifications and battle sites.
Duration

2 Hours

About

Journey through one of New Zealand’s most significant Maaori earthwork fortifications of the 1860s. The Rangiriri invasion by military forces is considered to be one of the bloodiest battles during the crown invasion of Waikato. Experience and walk through the world’s first Maaori replica earthwork trenches built to scale. Journey into the 19th century and learn about the Maaori spirit of resilience that led to the survival of Waikato and their ally tribes. Be moved by the narratives told by descendants of the men, women, and children who committed the ultimate act of selflessness. We survived annihilation and live to tell the stories.

The Experience
The Spirit of Resilience Public Tour experience includes visiting historic sites of significance including Te Maataarae ki Rangiriri (old paa site & military redoubt), the remnants of the original Rangiriri Paa earthworks and the world's first Maaori replica

Unforgettable Rotorua Adventure: Māori Culture, Hangi Feast, and Geothermal Wonders Await!
Tour Duration

13 hours

Tour Summary

Embark on an unforgettable private day tour from Auckland to explore the cultural and geothermal marvels of Rotorua. Dive into the heart of Māori traditions with an authentic hangi feast, mesmerizing cultural performances, and an exhilarating warrior canoe experience. The journey continues as you wander through the otherworldly landscapes of Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland, where bubbling hot springs, vibrant geothermal pools, and steaming geysers create a surreal and enchanting atmosphere.

Tour Highlights
Savor a picturesque drive through vibrant countryside, with plenty of stops and capture stunning photos.
Learn all about the fascinating culture only found in Aotearoa.
View the unveiling of traditional hangi meal.
Witness the dynamic scene of warriors on the waka taua (war canoe).
Enjoy the cultural Performance including Poi & Haka.
Insights into Māori heritage & historical uses of the land.
Learn about ancestral weapons and instruments.
Visit the thermal wonderland of Wai-O-Tapu and witness its unique volcanic features.
Walk through a surreal lunar-like environment to witness the volcanic wonders.
Carry on past the Champagne Pool, a bubbling hot spring famed for its brilliant orange rim.
Find a spot with the perfect mix of hot and cold temperatures, a truly magical experience.

Discover the heart and soul of Dunedin on this small-group tour designed to showcase the city’s most iconic sights. From striking architecture to sweeping coastal views, this tour blends rich history with vibrant local culture for a truly memorable day ashore.
Your journey begins with a warm welcome at Port Chalmers before heading into the city to explore highlights such as the stunning Dunedin Railway Station, picturesque Otago University, and the scenic St Clair Promenade. Take in panoramic views from Signal Hill Lookout and Unity Park, and snap a photo on Baldwin Street - the steepest street in the world.
You’ll enjoy some free time in The Octagon, Dunedin’s lively city centre, where you can explore, grab a bite, or simply soak up the local vibe.
A visit to Larnach Castle, New Zealand’s only castle, rounds out the day. You’ll have approximately an hour to wander at your own pace through the beautifully restored interiors and award-winning gardens with a self-guided tour.
After a full day of discovery, we’ll return you to your ship well in time for departure.
This tour is offered in partnership with one of our trusted local operators and delivered under the same high standards you can expect from Awesome Tours NZ.

About

You'll see the city, learn about our heritage, and experience the wild peninsula all in one comfortable and interesting tour. Our guide will commentate throughout the journey and there's plenty of time to chat and ask as many questions as you like. Dunedin is an ecotourism hub, and the attractions are suitable for visitors of any age. On our tours you will be able to access all the attractions, and you can explore as much or as little as you like. The tour takes in the Otago Peninsula and the Opera Eco -sanctuary reserve. Enjoy the stop off at Larnach Castle, New Zealand's only castle. Here you can buy lunch at the café and stroll the magnificent 14 hectares of gardens of international significance. Throughout the tour we'll make sure you have plenty of opportunity to photograph the wildlife and scenery.

Tour Details

We collect you and drop you off at your accommodation, cruise ship, or the Information Centre in the Octagon.

Picturesque and unforgettably beautiful
Duration

3.5 hours

About

Wellington is nestled around a sparkling bustling harbour with residential housing extending up the sides of green valley hills. Voted the most liveable city in the world in 2017 and 2018, you will not be disappointed with your tour of our lovely city.

This tour will delight you with breath-taking scenic views and photo opportunities, along with the fun of experiencing first-hand some of our favourite tourist attractions. You will be picked up from your meeting point and we will continue downtown past Te Papa and the QT hotel, seeing St Gerard’s church and monastery before we drive up to the top of Mount Victoria to see spectacular views of Wellington’s city and harbour. Have your cameras ready!

We now drive to Lyall Bay beach, pass Wellington airport and see some of the rugged coast line. On a clear day you may see some spectacular views of the South Island. We pass Barrett’s Reef, where on 10 April 1968 the roll on/roll off ferry Wahine hit the reef and capsized near Seatoun (just a short distance from the shoreline) during a hurricane resulting in 52 lives being lost.

name: He Toi Tupu - Education Tour
description:
2 Hour Guided Tour • Culturally Significant Battle Site and Replica Trenches

4.7 
stars
 

202 Google reviews

Exclusive for ECE, Primary, Intermediate & High School Only
Duration

2 Hours

Minimum Number of 25 students for any group Booking

About

The He Toi Tupu Education Tour is tailored to pre-school, primary, intermediate and secondary schools. This experience will take schools on a journey through one of New Zealand’s most significant Maaori earthwork fortifications of the 1860s.

The Rangiriri invasion by military forces is considered to be one of the bloodiest battles during the crown invasion of Waikato. This tour aligns with Aotearoa New Zealand's Histories and Te Takanga o te Waa and provides opportunities for teachers to engage in alternative methods to deliver their Histories curriculum. Through this experience, teachers, leaders and aakonga will gain a better understanding of a historical event that has impacted and shaped our present-day lives of Waikato and the Kiingitanga.

Walk through the world’s first Maaori replica earthwork trenches built to scale. Journey into the 19th century and learn about the Maaori spirit of resilience that led to the survival of Waikato and their ally tribes. Be moved by the narratives told by descendants of the men, women, and children who committed the ultimate act of selflessness. We survived annihilation and live to tell the stories.

The Experience
He Toi Tupu Education Tour includes visiting historic sites of significance
highlights: Dress for the weather as your tour is outdoors.
Have a snack and stay hydrated prior to arrival.
Restrictions regarding flash photography and video recordings.
Be sunsmart, wear hats for sun protection and sunscreen.
Wear comfortable enclosed shoes. We advise against wearing jandals, sandals, slip-on and high heels.
Merchandise is available for purchase, stocks are limited
Some limitations for wheelchair accessibility in the trenches

Oneke is a culturally significant area of Rakiura Stewart Island. This small-group, guided walk combines coastal and native bush trails with stories of Rakiura Māori and European history, early settlement, and connections to the land.

You'll experience native wildlife, learn about traditional plant use, and witness ongoing conservation and restoration efforts, all framed by a formal whakatau (Māori welcome) at the start of the journey.

You will head out to the Kawiti Glow Worm Cave, where a Kawiti Local Tour Guide will take you through an easy walk. With just a few steps. Experiencing the Galaxy of Glow worms on a 45-min tour through the Cave.

Then a short walk through the enchanted Forest of Kauri Trees of up to 800+ years old, settings of amazing nature.

Then off to the Hundertwasser Artistic Toilets & Centre, to see the most amazing N.Z Artist Creation.

For the Nature Adventure type of all ages!

About

Learn about our huge colourful hot springs, erupting geysers and active boiling mud pools, spectacular volcanoes and lush native forest. All on a fully guided 1/2 day tour in Rotorua. These are small group tours no more than 15 people. Medium level of fitness required.

Visit Rotorua’s most natural and popular geothermal parks.

Wai O Tapu
Waimangu Volcanic Valley
Boiling Mud Pools

The tour departs daily 1pm and returns 5pm. Pick up from accommodation then drive through forestry and farmland to NZ’s largest boiling mud pool. Your guide will then show you a series of colourful hot springs and volcanic features at Wai-o-tapu.

Next a short drive to the Waimangu Volcanic valley where your guide will explain the Mt Tarawera eruption which blew open this spectacular, bush covered, geothermal park, winner of NZ Eco Tourism Award 2001.

name: Iconic Otago Peninsula Wildlife Double
description:
Penguins and Albatross; Two Guided Wildlife Tours in One Day!

	342 Reviewers
#7 of 91 Tours in Dunedin
TripAdvisor Traveler Rating

Otago Peninsula Wildlife Tours
Rates

Adult (Ages 18+): $ 320
Child (Ages 16 & Under): $ 250

Duration

8 hours (Port Return)

start time may change to suit the arrival time of your cruise ship

Highlights

**This tour includes specialist guided tours at both Albatross and Penguin colonies **

Start promptly at 9:00 am from the ship's wharf at Port Chalmers (if your ship is scheduled for a later arrival we can customize the tour to suit). Travel along the harbour road to Dunedin City and head to the Otago Peninsula, home to the rare Yellow Eyed Penguins and majestic Royal Albatross. Experience the pristine views from up high along the top road of the Otago Peninsula. See plenty of sheep on the small farms and learn about the local Maori who have lived in Dunedin and on the Otago Peninsula for centuries. We arrive at Penguin Place for the first wildlife tour of the day at the Yellow Eyed Penguin Conservation Reserve. After the tour we have time for a light lunch before heading to Taiaroa Heads at the end of the Peninsula for the second wildlife tour at the Royal Albatross Colony. After completing both wildlife tours on the Otago Peninsula, we head back to Dunedin City for a brief city sights tour which includes a visit to Baldwin Street on route to the ship's wharf at Port Chalmers.

Penguin Plac

Prices for Friday, 1 May 2026 @ 9:30am. Prices include fees.

Take an exciting tour around Dunedin's historic city centre and the iconic Otago Peninsula. Enjoy a 6-hour tour as you see highlights of the city center, a diverse range of wildlife, and experience a 1 hour guided tour of the Royal Albatross Centre.
Duration

5-6 hours (Departure 9.00am from the Octagon or 9.30am from Port Chalmers cruise terminal - for cruise passengers)

Experience Dunedin and Otago Peninsula on a small group tour with plenty of flexibility and personalized attention. Begin your journey by meeting your tour guide between at the Octagon. Some highlights include the world's steepest street, viewpoints, and strolling around the botanic gardens.

After the city sightseeing, sit back to enjoy the most breathtaking scenery of Otago Peninsula along Highcliff Road with various stops for photo opportunities. After your 1-hour scenic drive, stop at the end of the Peninsula at The Royal Albatross Centre. Here you will immerse yourself in a 1-hour guided tour to learn all about Royal Albatross - one of the world's biggest seabirds.

The tour starts with a short introduction about The Royal Albatross before a short walk up a slight slope to the viewing platform to view Royal Albatross breeding colony.

name: Climbing & Bouldering - The Jurassic Climbs
description:
Lots of boulders, crags with top rope and lead climbs.

4.9 
stars
 

315 Google reviews

Overview
Duration
Varies
Location
567 Waipu Caves Road Waipu, North Island NZ 0582
Activity details

The Jurassic Climbs is the name of the bouldering and climbing crags at Waipu Caves Farm Park. The rock is hard limestone of good quality with fluted tops and flags on some edges.

THIS BOOKING IS FOR PEOPLE CLIMBING THEMSELVES WITH THEIR OWN EQUIPMENT. IT IS NOT A GUIDED OR SUPERVISED ACTIVITY.

There is about 75 routes spread across 3 crags 12m high and many boulders. With your own equipment you can top rope climb, lead climb, trad climb or boulder climb.

At The top of the crags there are hangers to use so you can easily access the pairs of hangers for top rope setup.

For trad climbing there are a few sections you could use your hardware.

Boulders have many boulders to pick from. You can hire mats at the office for $5 each per day.

The rock is kept clean so you can climb with maximum grip without the need for wire brushing moss or lichen.

The crags and boulders are spread around several spots and most are within 300m of the car park. With so many options we can handle about 60 climbers at a time.

You can see us listed on The Crag and Climb NZ under the name Waipu Caves Farm Park.

There is also a camp ground with toilets, hot showers, and fully equipped kitchen. There are snacks at the office.

If you are camping here you get free access to the climbing and bushwalking.

惠灵顿城市Eco徒步之旅

通过这场充满乐趣与知识的徒步之旅，探索惠灵顿的城市精华。行程从宏伟的新古典主义火车站出发，步行前往国会大厦，了解新西兰的政治文化；随后沿着惠灵顿的地震断层线前往植物园，在原生与外来植物交织的景观中，认识新西兰独特的自然生态。到达植物园顶端的缆车观景台后，我们将饱览城市美景、辨识地标，并了解惠灵顿的地形布局。随后乘坐缆车（含缆车单程票）到达繁华街区，穿越不同街道，最后抵达国家博物馆。

Activity details

行程从宏伟的新古典主义火车站出发，步行前往国会大厦，了解新西兰的政治文化；随后沿着惠灵顿的地震断层线前往植物园，在原生与外来植物交织的景观中，认识新西兰独特的自然生态。到达植物园顶端的缆车观景台后，我们将饱览城市美景、辨识地标，并了解惠灵顿的地形布局。随后乘坐缆车（含缆车单程票）到达繁华Lambton Quay街道并前往热闹的古巴街（Cuba Street）稍作休憩。行程最终在滨海区的国家博物馆结束。

What's included

含惠灵顿缆车单程车票

专业的司机和中文导游提供路线和景点的解说

Itinerary

惠灵顿火车站（Wellington Railway Station）
在火车站正门集合，导游欢迎并介绍行程。随后穿过气势恢宏的新古典主义建筑大厅，经地下通道前往国会大厦附近。
⏱ 约 15 分钟

新西兰蜂巢、国会大厦、议会图书馆建筑
在这里将介绍新西兰的政治体系与文化，以及惠灵顿作为首都所扮演的角色。同时讲解周边建筑的风格特色及其功能。
⏱ 约 15 分钟

惠灵顿植物园（Wellington Botanic Garden）
漫步于郁郁葱葱的花园、公共绿地与原生丛林之中，外来植物与本土动植物在此和谐共存。导游将讲解惠灵顿独特的生物多样性与自然环境。
⏱ 约 30 分钟

惠灵顿缆车站（Wellington Cable Car）

description:
Fox Glacier West Coast NZ

All Ages | 3 - 4 Hours | Informative guided tour in the Fox Glacier valley

	619 Reviewers
#10 of 109 Outdoor Activities in West Coast Region
TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
See magnificent Fox Glacier
Duration

3 hours (+ 1 hr driving time)

About

Embark on an unforgettable journey through the stunning landscapes of Fox Glacier. A gentle, gradual climb awaits, making this adventure accessible to all ages and fitness levels.

Departing at the Glacier Valley Eco Tours base in the township of Franz Josef Glacier - we’ll drive you to Fox Glacier from Franz Josef Glacier (a 30-minute drive) or if you are staying in Fox Glacier we'll pick you up from our meeting point in the Fox Glacier township (or your accommodation).

​This scenic trail meanders through the lush, historic glaciated rainforest of Westland Tai Poutini National Park, where you'll encounter magnificent rata and rimu trees, vibrant mosses, and breathtaking views of the upper Fox Glacier, the Fox Neve and Mt Tasman—one of New Zealand's tallest peaks. Along the way, our knowledgeable guides will share fascinating insights into the area's geology, glaciation, and thermology (including a chance to dip your hand in a geothermal spring) and its diverse flora and fauna.

​
Discover the rich history and local stories that make this region so unique. Join us for an educational and memorable hike that blends natural beauty with deep environmental unde

The Wild Wairoa River!!

This wild river is renowned for having some of the best white water action here in New Zealand.

A level up from the Kaituna this trip is not for the faint of heart, for this trip you need to have strong swimming skills and be up for an adventure!

Kaituna Cascades run the whole of the river including the bottom three rapids and the lower waterfall!
Hold on for dear life as you tackle ‘Mother's Nightmare’ which has a technical grade 4 rock garden, closely followed by a tight 2 metre drop!!

Experience the thrill of ‘The Rollercoaster’ – consisting of the aptly named ‘Devils Cauldron’ and the breathtaking ‘Bottom Drop’. Feel the rush of ‘The Waterfall’ which has an awesome 3 metre drop into a churning pool of white water through the Wairoa Rivers boulder-strewn rock canyon.

Activity details

Explore Oneke, a special area home to unique culture, beautiful coastal landscapes and wild kiwi.

Sea cruise: Scenic boat transfer to Oneke, a remote area only accessible by water.
Cultural welcome and Whare visit: Time in the Oneke whare (Māori meeting house) with storytelling, formal acknowledgement of the land, and cultural traditions.
Kiwi presentation: Learn about brown kiwi behaviour, conservation, and ongoing protection efforts.
Kiwi experience: Guided evening walk through native bush and along the coastline with the opportunity to see Stewart Island Brown kiwi in their natural habitat.
Wildlife encounters: Discover Ruru (morepork), sea lions, and other coastal species.
Conservation: Witness pest eradication and ongoing restoration efforts by Rakiura Māori.
Exclusive access: Explore Oneke, a heritage-rich and rarely visited area, ensuring a unique and immersive experience.

Duration

40 minutes

About

This 5* rated Mt Cook scenic flight includes a helicopter flight along the entire length of the mighty Tasman Glacier. All our helicopter tours include personal commentary from your pilot. They are passionate about their backyard and will show you the hidden treasures of this National Park that only a local expert can.

On this helicopter tour you will discover the real highlights of the Aoraki/Mt Cook National Park.

Fly the full length of the Haupapa/Tasman Glacier to appreciate the size and scale of New Zealand’s longest “river of ice”.

Get up close and personal with the mountain peaks and gain incredible views of the alpine region, including Aotearoa’s/New Zealand’s highest peak – Aoraki/Mt Cook – towering high in the sky at an incredible 3,724m tall.

In addition, you will view hanging glaciers, blue ice and rocky mountain formations, with photo opportunities in every direction.

Finally, touch down on the magnificent Haupapa/Tasman Glacier. Your pilot may shut off the helicopter so you can experience the ‘sound of silence’ – an unparalleled sensation of peace and tranquillity that you’ll never forget.

Overview

Activity details

Take off from Tauranga and discover the Coromandel from above on this unforgettable scenic flight. Soar over golden beaches, lush forests, and coastal highlights like Cathedral Cove and Hot Water Beach.

See one of New Zealand’s most beautiful regions from the air on this unforgettable scenic flight from Tauranga to the Coromandel Peninsula. Perfect for visitors wanting to explore more of the country’s natural beauty in a short time, this experience offers stunning coastal views and a unique aerial perspective on iconic landmarks such as the world-famous Cathedral Cove set of the Chronicles of Narnia.

Departing from Tauranga, you’ll fly over the sparkling waters of the Bay of Plenty, with incredible views of Mount Maunganui, Matakana Island, and the surrounding coastline. As you head north, the landscape opens up to reveal the rugged terrain of the Coromandel—famous for its secluded beaches, forested hills, and crystal-clear bays.

“The perfect way to try the sport of canyoning without the technical challenge”

This relaxed adventure gives you a taste of canyoning the Canyoning New Zealand way—without stepping into a full canyon environment—perfect for anyone keen to dip their toes into the sport while keeping things easy and enjoyable. You’ll abseil, slide, splash and jump down a huge rock wall designed to mimic the best parts of canyoning, all under the guidance of friendly pros who love sharing the thrill. Embrace the serene side of Waiheke Island on a journey that celebrates scenery, artistry, and taste. Begin your day with a peaceful hike along the Whakanewha Nikau Trail, where lush native bush, birdsong, and glimpses of the sea create a tranquil beginning to your adventure. As you walk beneath the elegant canopy of nīkau palms, absorb the soothing rhythm of nature and the calm energy that defines this beautiful corner of the island.

Continue your adventure with a visit to a boutique olive estate for an engaging olive oil tasting. Learn about the art of olive cultivation and sample a variety of rich, aromatic oils that express the island’s distinctive terroir. Each taste offers a glimpse into the craftsmanship and passion that define Waiheke’s local producers.

Next, delight in a gourmet lunch thoughtfully paired with fine local wines, each dish inspired by the island’s seasonal ingredients and relaxed coastal lifestyle. Savour the harmony of flavours while enjoying panoramic views that stretch across vineyards and sea.

In the afternoon, awaken your artistic side with a hands-o

TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
Come riding with us!
About

Enjoy a guided bike tour along the beautiful Karāpiro section of the Waikato River Trails.

Our guides let you know what's coming up, and share their knowledge and history of the area as you soak in the surroundings of extremely popular section of trail, which takes your through wetland to Arapuni Village.

The Waikato River Trails offer great views and spectacular flora and fauna only found in New Zealand.

Boasting more than 100km of Grade 2 and 3 riding (easy to intermediate), there is a trail for everyone- and we can take you on guided tour along the stunning Karāpiro section.

Starting at Arapuni Dam (best for beginners) or Jones Landing (for more intermediate riders), our guides will let you know what's coming up, and share their local knowledge and history of the area.

Meet at Riverside Adventures base on Horahora Road and hop on board a shuttle. You can use your own bike or hire one of ours - we have mountain bikes and e-bikes.

About

Our Blue Rider room in our funky horse themed homestead is the perfect choice for friends visiting or a room for the kids and provides a comfortable place to rest your head after a day of exploring Golden Bay.

House guests have access to large communal lounge & dining area, fully equipped kitchen, pizza oven, outdoor entertainment area and shared shower/bathroom facilities. There is limited wifi available (enough to make plans or check social media, but not enough to stream movies etc).

From our homestead you can follow the hack-track down to the beach, hang out with our team of horses, try our indoor climbing wall, go for a swim or swing down by the lake or even book a ride to make the most of your time with us.

We look forward to welcoming you to Hack Farm and creating a memorable holiday together.

Embark on an epic journey through Middle-earth in Wellington!
Duration

7 hours

About

This tour is more than just visiting the filming sites! On this informative tour, your guide will share fascinating little-known gems relating to the filming at each site, that will make you want to watch the films all over again!

From your hotel or i-Site pickup we make our way to the Hutt Valley. The first stop is the Dry Creek Quarry where you see the filming site of Helm’s Deep and Minas Tirith.

A short drive will see us stop at the River Anduin (Hutt River) where the Fellowship travelled to Amon Hen.

Then it is on to the site of Gardens of Isengard (Harcourt Park) where Gandalf met Saruman the White to discuss the Ring, and it is also where Saruman’s Orc army destroy the gardens.

Next stop is Rivendell (Kaitoke Regional Park) the home of the Elves. Enjoy a walk through Rivendell and see the nearby river used for the scene where Éomer recovers the dying Théodred, son of King Théoden. More adventurous travellers may also cross the swing bridge over the Hutt River.

Now we are off to the Weta Workshop in Miramar. Here you have an exclusive behind-the-scenes tour (entry included in your tour price) where you will see how props plus weaponry are made and possibly mee
